_meta.description: Conservative regional engineering defaults — NOT verified extracts from a specific published Uganda code or hazard map. Use as a STARTING POINT only; every project requires a proper site investigation, the local authority's approved wind / seismic / soil values, and a structural engineer's sign-off.

accuracy_caveats.wind: Basic vb,0 (10-min mean at 10m, terrain Cat II) is an engineering estimate from typical East African meteorology — NOT extracted from a published Uganda wind map. Northern values (Gulu / Lira 30 m/s) are on the high side and should be calibrated against actual met-station data when available.
accuracy_caveats.seismic: Peak ground acceleration agR/g is plausible for the East African Rift but NOT cited from the Uganda NEMA earthquake risk profile or a peer-reviewed Uganda hazard study. Return period is unstated; treat as approximate 475-year EC8 design hazard. For collapse-prevention design (2475-year) the values may need to be scaled up.
accuracy_caveats.soil_bearing: Region-level averages — useless without a site investigation. Kampala has good lateritic on hills and expansive black-cotton clay in valleys (Kololo / Bukoto bottoms etc.); the 150 kPa default applies to the firm-lateritic case only. Same risk in any Ugandan urban valley.
accuracy_caveats.rainfall: 5-yr return intensity values are typical East African climate norms, NOT extracted from the Uganda Department of Meteorology IDF curves. Use site-specific IDF when available.
accuracy_caveats.ground_type_q_factor: EC8 ground type (A-E) and behaviour factor q are NOT captured here. Notes mention soil class loosely. Both must be set per project for any meaningful seismic design force calculation.

intended_use: Quick start when no project-specific load study has been done yet
Sanity-check baseline against which engineer-supplied values can be compared
Pre-design exploration of which regions are seismic-critical vs wind-critical
do_not_use_for: Final-design seismic forces without consulting Uganda NEMA hazard maps
Foundation design without a site investigation report
Storm sizing for return periods other than ~5 years without local IDF curves
Any claim of code compliance — these are starting points, not certified values
references_to_consult_per_project: Uganda National Building Code 2010 (regulatory; STING does not include extracts)
Uganda NEMA Earthquake Risk Profile / OECD Uganda Disaster Risk Profile 2017
BS EN 1991-1-4 NA — wind, with terrain category cs/cd and ce(z) per project
BS EN 1998-1 EC8 — seismic, with ground type S, q-factor and importance class γi
BS EN 1997-1 EC7 — soils, with site investigation per BS 5930
Uganda Department of Meteorology IDF curves (project-specific)

regions.label: Kampala / Central (Wakiso, Mukono, Entebbe)
regions.notes: Lake-Victoria buffered wind; moderate seismic; lateritic / red clay subgrade typical.

regions.label: Jinja / Eastern lake-edge
regions.notes: Lakefront industrial corridor; alluvial pockets near Nile.

regions.label: Mbale / Tororo / Eastern (Mt Elgon)
regions.notes: Higher wind from Mt Elgon orographic effects; firmer volcanic subgrade.

regions.label: Gulu / Northern (Acholi)
regions.notes: Open savannah, higher unobstructed wind; low seismic — off the rift.

regions.label: Lira / Northern (Lango)
regions.notes: Similar to Gulu — open terrain, low seismic.

regions.label: Mbarara / Southwestern
regions.notes: Elevated plateau; rift-edge seismic risk begins to rise.

regions.label: Kabale / Kisoro / Southwestern highlands
regions.notes: On the East African Rift's western branch; significant seismic hazard.

regions.label: Kasese / Bundibugyo / Western (Rwenzori)
regions.notes: Highest seismic hazard in Uganda — directly on the rift western branch; firm volcanic subgrade.

regions.label: Fort Portal / Kabarole / Western
regions.notes: Rift-edge; firm volcanic subgrade; high seismic.

regions.label: Arua / West Nile
regions.notes: Northwestern semi-arid; lower seismic — west of rift.

regions.label: Soroti / Eastern (Teso)
regions.notes: Black-cotton soil pockets — check expansion / shrinkage limits.

regions.label: Custom (engineer-defined)
regions.notes: Conservative Kampala-equivalent fallback. Override every value on a per-project site investigation.

occupancy_live_loads_kpa._meta: EC1-1-1 Cat A-E / Uganda NBC; override BLE_LIVE_LOAD_KPA per occupancy
